commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8acbb21f556d9ba9fcb089afdb5a102e5aab05df)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a Windows Node 24.0-24.14 compile-cache skip to `src/entry.compile-cache.ts` plus focused unit coverage for that helper.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +103. Total +134 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence native Windows reproduction was run in this review. Source inspection does give a high-confidence patch repro: the shipped package bin still enters `openclaw.mjs`, and that file remains unguarded in the PR head.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review and contributor action are needed because the code must be moved to the shipped launcher path and real Windows package proof remains a merge gate.

target.title: fix(cli): skip compile cache on early Node 24.x to avoid startup deadlock
target.body:
## Summary

Disable Node compile cache only for native Windows packaged installs on early Node 24.x versions (< 24.15), where the linked issue reports an ESM loader startup deadlock.

This keeps compile cache enabled for non-Windows packaged installs on Node 24.0-24.14, and for Windows packaged installs on Node 24.15+ and other Node majors.

Fixes #86550

## Root Cause

- Root cause: OpenClaw enables Node's compile cache in the entry path before importing the full CLI. On native Windows npm-global installs with early Node 24.x, the linked issue reports a startup hang at that entry stage. The source of the failure is the entry-level `enableCompileCache(...)` decision before command dispatch, not gateway, plugin, channel, provider, or command-specific code.
- Why this is root-cause fix: `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache(...)` is the single source-of-truth gate for whether OpenClaw calls Node's compile-cache API during packaged CLI startup. The patch blocks that call only when both parts of the reported failure boundary are present: `process.platform === "win32"` and Node 24 minor < 15. That removes the reported entry-level deadlock trigger for Windows while preserving the existing compile-cache contract elsewhere.
- Fix classification: root-cause startup gate fix with an explicit proof gap for native Windows npm-global after-fix behavior.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: Medium-high for the source boundary and regression tests; medium overall because maintainer-side or authorized native Windows npm-global proof is still required before merge.
- Why it matters / User impact: If the reported hang reproduces, affected Windows users cannot get basic CLI commands like `openclaw --version`, `openclaw doctor --fix`, or `openclaw gateway start` to exit. The update protects that availability path without taking compile-cache performance away from macOS/Linux users on the same Node minors.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: native Windows npm-global CLI startup can hang on Node 24.1.0 when Node compile cache is enabled before the CLI import path.
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 local checkout with focused unit and type checks for the entry compile-cache decision.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:core:test`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: The focused Vitest shard passed 18 tests. The tests prove `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache({ nodeVersion: "24.1.0", platform: "win32", installRoot: <pkg> })` returns `false`; Node 24.0-24.14 on `linux` / `darwin` returns `true`; and Windows Node 24.15+ returns `true`.
- Observed result after fix: The compile-cache skip is scoped to Windows early Node 24 instead of disabling compile cache for every platform on Node 24.0-24.14.
- What was not tested: No native Windows npm-global after-fix run was available in this environment, so `openclaw --version`, `openclaw doctor --fix`, and `openclaw gateway start` were not executed on Windows + Node 24.1.0. Local Linux gateway proof is not a substitute because the requested proof is native Windows packaged CLI startup before gateway-specific behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: early Node 24.x disables compile cache on Windows packaged installs, stays enabled for early Node 24.x on Linux/macOS packaged installs, and stays enabled for Node 24.15+ / Node 22 / Node 25 on Windows packaged installs.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the changed behavior is a pure startup decision before command execution; injecting `nodeVersion` and `platform` directly proves the decision boundary without requiring the test runner itself to run on each OS and Node minor.

## Scope and architecture

- What did NOT change: no respawn logic, gateway behavior, plugin/channel/provider code, config surface, dependency, lockfile, docs, or unrelated startup path changed. This is out of scope for unrelated CLI startup hangs outside Windows packaged early-Node-24 compile-cache startup.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache(...)` remains the canonical entry compile-cache owner. The new platform and Node-version check lives beside the existing source-checkout and `NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE` gates, so callers do not need duplicate runtime/platform policy.
- Compatibility / related open PR scan: the compatibility-sensitive surface is packaged CLI startup defaulting, and this patch reduces the current PR's blast radius. `check-upstream-fixed` reported current `origin/main` does not already cover this PR, with no overlapping main changes in `src/entry.compile-cache.ts` or `src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`.

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-002 / RF-003 / RF-006: Still require native Windows npm-global proof for Node 24.0-24.14. This update does not claim that proof; it keeps the proof gap explicit.
- RF-004: Fixed by narrowing the runtime guard to Windows, so macOS/Linux packaged installs on Node 24.0-24.14 keep compile cache enabled.
- RF-005: Maintainer decision is still needed for final Windows proof acceptance, but the code no longer makes the broader all-platform tradeoff.
- RF-007: Covered by focused tests proving Windows Node 24.15+ still allows compile cache.
- RF-008: Covered by `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`.

## Patch quality notes

- Patch quality notes: The added `return false` is the intentional safe branch for the affected Windows early-Node-24 runtime: do not call Node's compile-cache API when it can hang before any CLI command logic runs. The default for every other packaged runtime remains the existing compile-cache-enabled path. No new dependencies, config knobs, fallback stacks, or runtime shims were added.

## Merge risk

- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: availability`, `merge-risk: compatibility`.
- Risk explanation: Availability risk is the reported Windows startup hang. Compatibility/performance risk is changing the packaged startup compile-cache default. The updated patch limits that default change to Windows Node 24.0-24.14 only.
- Why acceptable: Compile cache is a startup optimization, while the reported failure prevents the CLI from starting at all. Preserving compile cache outside the reported Windows early-Node-24 boundary keeps the fix narrow and makes the remaining tradeoff explicit for maintainers to validate with native Windows proof.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
